english: pa to recover one's senses Pth.; ysó - ba (gso ba) id.; dran - ₒdzin - méd - pa (dran 'dzin med pa) being out of one's senses (with joy) Glr.; self-possession, consideration, drán - méd (dran med) without consideration, inconsiderate; sėms - ċan smyón - pa - dag drán - pa so - sór rnyed (sems can smyon pa dag dran pa so sor rnyed) insane persons regained the respective faculties of their minds S.O., drán - pa yzúṅs - pa (dran pa gzungs pa) prob. quickness of apprehension, good capacity; drán - pa nyáms - pa (dran pa nyams pa) weak-minded; dran - yód (dran yod), dran - ldán (dran ldan), remembering, being in one's senses Cs.; dran - śés (dran shes) for drán - pa daṅ śes - ráb (dran pa dang shes rab) Mil.; *ḍhem - pa maṅ - po ǩo - la śar* (*dhem pa mang po kho la shar*) C. he is uneasy, troubled, full of scruples and apprehensions.
1. v. lċam - mo (lcam mo). 2. v. ₒdral - ba ('dral ba). 3. for gral (gral).
a kind of courier or messenger Cs.

col. also drí - ma (dri ma), odour, smell, scent, dri - żím(-po) (dri zhim(-po)), dri - bsúṅ (dri bsung) Dzl. an agreeable smell, sweet scent; dri - bzáṅ(-po) (dri bzang(-po)) 1. id., 2. Cs. also saffron; dri - ṅán (dri ngan), prob. also dri - lóg (dri log), W. *dri sóg - po* (*dri sog po*), Cs. dri - mi - żím (dri mi zhim) an unpleasant smell, a stench; dri bró - ba (dri bro ba) to exhale an odour Glr.; *ḍi núm - pa* (*di num pa*) or *nóm - pa* (*nom pa*) to inhale an odour; W.: *kyúr - ḍi* (*kyur di*), *nyíṅ - ḍi* (*nying di*), *dúg - ḍi* (*dug di*), *mé - ḍi* (*me di*), *rúl - ḍi* (*rul di*), *hám - ḍi rag* (*ham di rag*) I perceive a sour, stale, burnt, smoky, putrid, mouldy smell; *tsíg - ḍi* (*tsig di*), *żob - ḍi* (*zhob di*) a smell of burnt food, burnt wool; dri lṅa (dri lnga) five odours or perfumes used in offering; dri - ǩa (dri kha) Sch.: urinous smell (?); dri - ṅád (dri ngad) vapour, exhalation, fragrance; dri - ċan lté - ba bag (dri can lte ba bag) of the musk - deer; musk Wdṅ.; dri - ču (dri chu) scented water, perfume Cs. (yet cf. drí - ma (dri ma)), dri - čén (dri chen) a medicinal herb Lt. — dri - ytsaṅ - ǩáṅ (dri gtsang khang), गन्धकुटी, a sacred place, a chapel, conjectures about the etymology of the word v. Burn. I, 262. — dri - ₒdzín - po (dri 'dzin po), the nose. — dri - za (dri za), also dri - za - mo (dri za mo) fem., गन्धर्व an eater of fragrance, in Brahmanism the heavenly musicians, and so also in Buddhism painted as playing on guitars, but usually (in accordance with the etymology) thought to be aërial spirits, that feed on odours of every description. They are supposed not only to be fond of flowers and other fragrant objects, but also to visit dunghills, flaying-places, shambles etc., the various substances of which are accordingly dedicated to them (cf. ytór - ma (gtor ma)). The insects, swarming about such places, the Tibetan believes to be incarnated dri - za (dri za). — dri - zai groṅ(-ǩyer) (dri za'i grong(-khyer)) mirage, fata morgana.

question, dri-ba ₒdri-ba (dri ba 'dri ba) to ask a question, mi-la (mi la) a person; dri-bai lan (dri ba'i lan), dris - lán (dris lan), answer; dri - rtóg ma maṅ (dri rtog ma mang) Mil., C., *ḍhi gya ma jhé or čé* (*dhi gya ma jhe or che*) Cs., don't ask long! do not ask many questions!
an enchanter, sorcerer, magician. dri-mo (dri mo) enchantress, witch Mil.

मल, 1. dirt, filth, impurity; excrement, ordure; lag(-pai) dri(-ma) (lag(-pa'i) dri(-ma)) marks left by dirty fingers on books etc.; sná-dri (sna dri) mucus, snot, snivel S.g.; dri-ma ysum (dri ma gsum) the three impurities, excrement, urine, sweat; but sometimes more are enumerated; frq. fig.: nyés-pai (nyes pa'i), nyon-móṅs-pai (nyon mongs pa'i), ǩa-na-ma - tó-bai drí-ma (kha na ma tho ba'i dri ma); drí-ma kun zád-nas (dri ma kun zad nas) after all impurities have been put off Dzl.; dri-ču (dri chu) 1. urine, ₒdór - ba ('dor ba) to urinate Glr.; rés-ₒga raṅ-byuṅ-gi drí-ču sten (res 'ga' rang byung gi dri chu sten) sometimes (in my extremity) I had recourse to my own water Mil. — 2. v. sub dri (dri). — dri-čén (dri chen) feces of the intestinal canal. — dri-ma - ċan (dri ma can) dirty, sluttish, as to dress; drí-ma-méd-pa (dri ma med pa) clean, cleanly. — 2. for dkri-ma (dkri ma), v. dkri-ba (dkri ba).

Cs. = 'drin ('drin) kindness, favour; yet, yżan driṅ mi ₒjog (gzhan dring mi 'jog) Lex., yżán - gyis driṅ - la mi ₒjog - ċiṅ raṅ - gi ċos żugs-so (gzhan gyis dring la mi 'jog cing rang gi chos zhugs so) Dom.? One dictionary renders it by प्रत्यय, knowledge; certainty, faith, confidence

resp. bka-drín (bka' drin), rarely sku-drin (sku drin) Glr., kindness, favour, grace, blá - mai drin - gyis (bla ma'i drin gyis) by the grace of my Lama, of my spiritual father, of my patron saint Mil.; in addressing a person, kyed (khyed) (or kyod (khyod))-kyi bka-drín-gyis (kyi bka' drin gyis) is gen. used; mai drin (ma'i drin) benefits conferred by a mother Thgy.; drín-ċan (drin can), drin - čé (drin che) kind, gracious, benevolent; benefactor, drín - ċan pa - má (drin can pha ma) the parents, these benefactors; drín-ċan már-pa (drin can mar pa), Marpa <
